question: Does the COMPOSITION of ADR-036..043 (the fully-assembled threeway control plane), audited holistically rather than per-fix, admit an emergent forged-promotion or merge-DoS that the per-ADR certification passes (each scoped to one fix's diff) never looked for?

method.shape: 5 map-slice readers (barrier) -> 5 adversarial find lenses -> 3-skeptic adversarial refute per candidate -> synthesis
method.r_verify_tier: genuinely-new question stated BEFORE launch (whole-system composition, not re-run of any single ADR cert)

key_findings: Drove the REAL gate (run_gate + verify_and_reduce via the seatkit signing fixture) end-to-end on a full T3 set for the FIRST time — no committed test does this — and it merged correctly.

Strongest cross-fix attack class (ADR-036/037 revoke+collision machinery turned against ADR-043's NEW overseer singletons re_verify_challenge and approver_roster) was driven through the real gate and FAILS CLOSED in every case: _revoke_authorized gates the two new singletons because their ids enter seat_by_id as overseer-only; non-overseer revoke ignored; colliding-id squat blocked by store.append EventIdCollision or fails closed with GateError per ADR-037.

Both prior INFO candidates independently re-refuted: no forged-promotion bypass across the composition; the 'build-pipeline seat doubles as approver' concern is a roster-CONSTRUCTION policy question for the overseer (same class as ADR-043's documented nonce-rotation precondition), NOT a code gap — confirmed by driving run_gate with operator2 (the T2 mirror co-signer) ALSO rostered as a T3 human: merges with two genuinely-distinct key-bound seats, as designed.
attack_classes_exercised_through_real_ga: ADR-037 colliding-id squat on a new ADR-043 singleton
ADR-036-revoke x ADR-039-assignment x T2-mirror-cosign break (insider AND overseer)
not_covered_no_silent_caps: cutover.py / divergence.py / legacy_projector.py / cursor_backfill.py — Slice-2.5 migration + one-shot authority-flip substrate, NOT driven (structurally disjoint from the promotion-authority surface — legacy carriers project to the non-load-bearing event_sent kind, never reduced, no write path by construction — so it cannot host a forged-promotion composition gap, BUT its one-shot authority-FLIP + partial-bus-teardown correctness is its own concern for the LIVE CUTOVER).
refstore.py REMOTE push-CAS append loop + idempotency-dedup-verify under concurrent multi-writer contention (tests use the local EventStore only).
multi-candidate / cross-pair concurrent run_gate interleaving (every probe was single-candidate single-invocation).
overseer key-compromise blast radius (out of trust model by design — overseer is the root for roster/challenge/assignment/release; ADR-043 narrows but does not eliminate it).
gitcas merge-tree/commit-tree determinism under tree states beyond the clean-merge and textual-conflict fixtures.
implication: The promotion/authority CORE of the assembled control plane is certified clean at the composition level (first-ever end-to-end real-gate T3 merge). This is a positive input to the live-cutover GO/NO-GO. It does NOT certify the cutover substrate itself (cutover.py one-shot flip + divergence + legacy projector), which is the explicit residual for the next pass before any flip.
